opps.title: HHS ASFR - Diabetes Health Marketing and Communications Support Services - MRAS
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Financial Resources
opps.description:
On behalf of Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), GSA is conducting market research.

DO NOT submit your response in eBuy. Your response will not be included in the report that is provided to the customer. NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the below link into a new browser.

Only responses submitted through the linked RFI will be accepted. Nothing else will be evaluated or recorded (this includes selecting 'interested' within eBuy).


Link to RFI: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_3sni2cOmQrTivb0


Additional Information:
Due to the structure of contracts in GSA eBuy, you may receive multiple Sources Sought or RFI notifications for the same requirement if it falls under multiple of your company's awarded SINs. However, please note that you are only required to respond to the RFI once.

Be sure to use the URL provided above to submit your response.


Any questions regarding this RFI will be responded to by email only.  Send your inquiry to: rfi@research.gsa.gov and be sure to include the RFQ number.
opps.summary:

The goal is to gather market research to identify potential contractors who can provide effective support services in this area. The GSA is conducting this market research on behalf of HHS.

 The contractor will be responsible for providing comprehensive marketing and communication support services related to diabetes health initiatives. This includes developing a detailed methodology and approach for organizing, staffing,

opps.title: Request for Information (RFI) School Choice Education Voucher Platform
opps.description:
This request for information (RFI) is issued solely for information and planning and does not constitute a request for quote (RFQ) or a promise to issue an RFQ in the future.

The Department of War Education Activity (DoWEA) seeks information on how an interested contractor could provide a commercially available, proven platform to administer proposed school choice education voucher program. This program is expected to support approximately 11,000 dependents of military personnel located at US bases/installations.

DoWEA anticipates making one award for a one base year and four option years. The anticipated NAICS code for this requirement is 518210 (Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services).  Note: Release of a formal solicitation is contingent on the enactment of pending legislative authority and appropriation of funds.   

Please see attached Request for Information (RFI) for more details.

opps.title: Visual Arts Support Services.
opps.description:
The U.S. Fire Administration (USFA), located at 16825 S. Seton Avenue, Emmitsburg, Maryland, requires visual arts support to provide professional graphic design services for USFA and NDEMU staff. The graphic and multimedia design staff will be located onsite at USFA.

Design staff will create visual assets that highlight and elevate USFAâ€™s brand, reinforcing its position as a trusted leader in fire and emergency medical services. Visual arts support services are expected to recommend best design practices.

The government will provide all necessary graphic design software and material supplies.

Design staff responsibilities include:

Publication layout and digital formatting
Training materials
Factsheets, handouts, flyers, and infographics
Illustrations
3D and 2D animations and simulations
Animated and motion graphics video productions
Program graphics and posters
Production work (large format printing, laminating, etc.)
Limited studio photography
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
Strong multimedia/visual comms match — BLN24 proven prime (CDC NCHS $3.6M, USDA social media, DOI video)
8(a) set-aside — direct vehicle match for BLN24
⚠️ Gap area detected: Training & eLearning — would need a teaming partner for this scope
matched_lanes: Multimedia/Visual Comms (CDC NCHS $3.6M, USDA, DOI — proven prime)

tasks.note: BLN24 prime — CDC NCHS visual comms $3.6M, DOI/USDA video work
BLN24 prime — CDC NCHS visual comms, MBDA branding, USDA social media graphics
BLN24 has production/visual design experience; large format print is adjacent
BLN24 gap — not a service lane

capability_matrix.fit_reason: Majority of task areas require teaming — may not be best lead

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP

opps.title: One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside Continuously Open Solicitation
opps.description:
May 12, 2026 The U.S. General Services Administration’s Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD) is pleased to announce the first Phase II apparent awardee notifications for OASIS+ 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside. A consolidated list of Phase II apparent awardees for all OASIS+ contract families may be accessed on the Solicitations (continuously open) page (see “Links” section within this SAM.gov posting). This list will be updated with additional apparent awardees as award announcements continue to be made.

Award notifications have been sent via email to offerors who have been determined successful in one or more proposed domains. Apparent awardees are reminded to ensure that their SAM.gov entity information is active, accurate, and complete at all times. The program plans to issue formal awards and notices to proceed (NTPs) to qualified contractors on or about May 22, 2026 after final legal reviews occur. The newly awarded contracts will be available for ordering in GSA eBuy one business day following the issuance of formal awards and NTPs and upon contractor registration within the Vendor Support Center (VSC), whichever is later. Contract awardees must register their contracts in VSC before they will be visible in eBuy and available for procurement. 

Please note that GSA continues to release answers to RFP questions deemed broadly beneficial and relevant on a regular basis on the program website Solicitations (continuously open) page. Questions related to the award announcements should be submitted via email to the OASIS+ Contracting Officers at oasisplusmods@gsa.gov.

Please continue to monitor this OASIS+ Interact Community forum where you can join and subscribe to get notifications about announcements and program updates. For more information about the OASIS+ Program, visit: The OASIS+ website. Also, follow our social media channels X and LinkedIn. 

************************************************************************************

opps.title: Decennial Transformation & Application Modernization Acquisition
opps.description: The CenTAM Acquisition Framework supports the Business Ecosystem, the Decennial (2030 Census and early 2040 Census research), as well as IT, economic, geographic, demographic, research methodology programs, and other program areas within USCB. The purpose is to plan, develop, document, test, train, monitor, manage, and maintain software architecture and applications to support the 2030 Decennial Census operations. DTAM is the modernization of existing applications and the development of new applications, across Decennial systems, that will be leveraged for the Decennial Census Programs, in support of the 2028 Census Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census.
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
Partial cloud/modernization match — Clarity24 JV has NOAA cloud delivery experience
Small business set-aside

pp_refs: NOAA NWS IDP Cloud Transition / App Mod (Clarity24, $7M)
tasks.note: BLN24 JV — Clarity24 NOAA app mod $2.8M, Census MAF/TIGER modernization

capability_matrix.fit_reason: Can prime most: Application Modernization. Some areas need partner

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$100.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Census Bureau's massive digital modernization initiative — the CenTAM framework — covering IT systems for the 2030 Decennial Census. Includes application development, legacy modernization, cloud migration, data collection platforms, and systems to support economic, geographic, and demographic programs. Multiple separate contract awards expected under this framework.
rex_analysis.rex_take: Clarity24 JV has direct Census past performance ($4.5M MAF/TIGER modernization, $4.5M EDL Applications, $2.8M NOAA app mod). Census is also BLN24's highest-value client ($18.4M). This is a real opportunity — but it's a large technology modernization vehicle, not comms or HCD. The question is which specific task areas under CenTAM align with what Clarity24 can actually deliver vs. what larger primes will own. Full & Open means big integrators will compete.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Get into the weeds on the CenTAM acquisition structure — it's a collection of complementary contracts, not one big award. Find which specific package maps to digital modernization vs. IT infrastructure vs. data engineering. Clarity24 should be positioned for the digital/app mod pieces. Due 2027 — shape now.

opps.title: 16WS Cloud Platform BPA
opps.description:
55 CONS is conducting a Sources Sought to determine the interest and availability of vendors for a Firm Fixed Price (FFP) Blanket Purchase agreement for Cloud Service Provider (CSP) credits set forth within the attached Statement of Need (SON).

We are intending to award this as a competative Brand Name requirement with a certified Amazon Web Services reseller.  Offerors may submit alternate "equal to" proposals for consideration by the governement.

Salient Characteristics must include:

-The company must be able to provide Cloud Service Provider (CSP) capabilities in regions designated for US Government only use and commercial.

-Those CSPs must have the capability to host Impact Level 6 (IL6) based on the DoD Cloud Computing Security Requirements Guide (SRG).

-The company must be authorized to offer capabilities in those US Government only regions up to IL6. 

- The company must be capable of providing payment services (credits) on behalf of wholly governement operated accounts. 

Please review attached Statement of Need and submit capabilities packages/letters of interest to Mr. Casey Hupton at casey.hupton.1@us.af.mil and TSgt Jennifer Prestridge at jennifer.prestridge.1@us.af.mil.

opps.title: Updated: Enterprise Remote Document Authentication
opps.description:
*** 5/19/2026: USCIS anticipates releasing a solicitation on NASA SEWP V set-aside to small business and anticipates solicitation release in early June. This requirement will be competed without any brand-name specifications.***

USCIS anticipate a requirement for a commercial software solution for document authentication and identity verification service. These services are essential forÂ maintainingÂ program integrity, supporting federal operations, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Historically, many USCIS processes have relied on manual, in-person document review, which can present operational challenges and limit access and flexibility,Â especially for remote or distributed users. More specifically, the solution will capture high quality images of identity and work authorization documents, apply Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) and face match checks, use template and barcode/MRZ based authentication, and extract structured data via Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for use in USCIS-controlled microservices. All processing will occur within USCIS or approved Department of Homeland Security (DHS) environments, in compliance with DHS security and privacy requirements.

opps.title: Facilities Support Services
opps.description:
Please see attached RFQ 70RFP326QEH000009 for detailed description and requirements. Attachments are paperclipped to the RFQ PDF. 

The purpose of this solicitation is to request quotations from contract holders under the One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) Total Small Business Set-Aside, an Indefinite-Delivery, Indefinite-Quantity (IDIQ) Multi-Agency Contract (MAC) category #10101 for NAICS 541611 under the Management and Advisory. This procurement is conducted in accordance with RFO FAR 16.507 and will be solicited via GSA eBuy.

The subsequent award will provide contractor support services for the Federal Protective Service (FPS) Administrative Services Division (ASD). This requirement supports the agency in administering space planning, relocation coordination, build-out and reconfiguration project management, occupancy agreement tracking and planning, and strategic real estate support advisory services across its portfolio. Included along with this RFQ are the following attachments:

-Attachment A: Performance Work Statement (PWS)
-Attachment B: Task Order Clauses
-Attachment C: Price Element Breakdown
-Attachment D: Task Order Provisions & Evaluation Criteria
-Attachment E: Past Project Forms
-Attachment F: Past Performance Questionnaires

All contractor quotations must be prepared in accordance with the Performance Work Statement (PWS) and the contractual terms and conditions outlined in this RFQ and its attachments. Quotations should be valid for a minimum of 120 days. Offerors may be requested to provide uncertified cost or pricing data to support price realism & IAW FAR 15.404-1 to ensure that the final agreed-to-price is fair and reasonable. A small business subcontracting plan is not required for this effort.

Contractors must submit labor hour rates for positions relevant to the areas of expertise required, using OASIS+ standardized labor categories and seniority levels (e.g., Junior, Journeyman, Senior, SME) as established in

opps.description: Graphic Designer ServicesThis is a combined synopsis/solicitation for a commercial product and services prepared in accordance with the format in Subpart 12.201-1, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This RFQ constitutes the only solicitation; quotations are being requested and a written solicitation is being issued, which can be found on www.sam.gov. The solicitation number is 140G0126Q0141 and is issued as a Request for Quotation (RFQ). This RFQ is set aside for small business concerns.The US Geological Survey (USGS) is requesting discounted open-market firm-fixed price quotation packages for graphic designer services, in accordance with the Statement of Work located in RFQ. The posted RFQ contains the instructions to vendors, Statement of Work, applicable clauses, and additional attachments. All vendors who can meet this requirement have a valid SAM UEI Number, and are registered in the System for Award Management are invited to submit electronic quotes for this posted RFQ. All questions shall be submitted via email to the Contract Specialist, Patrick Anderson, at Patrick_anderson@ios.doi.gov no later than 01:00PM Eastern, Thursday, June 4, 2026. Please include the solicitation number in the subject.  Quotation packages are due via email to the Contract Specialist, Patrick Anderson, at Patrick_anderson@ios.doi.gov no later than 1:00 PM Eastern, Wednesday, June 10, 2026.
opps.summary:

This contract aims to maintain a professional design standard that enhances recognition and trust in USGS products.

Amendment 0001, June 3 2026

The due date for proposals has been extended to 4:00 PM Eastern Time, on June 10, 2026. 

Description 

This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products or commercial services prepared in accordance with FAR parts 12 and 15. This combined synopsis constitutes the only solicitation. Solicitation number 47QACA26R0274 is issued as a request for proposals (RFP) for a Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) for  loan file review services. 

This acquisition is set-aside for small business concerns. This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov. 

In accordance with FAR 12.202(b)(1) the following information is being included in this synopsis/solicitation. 

A list of line-item number(s) and items, quantities, and units of measure (including option(s), if applicable). 

See Attachment 1 Offer For Standard Services/Section B Pricing Sheet 

Description of requirements for the items to be acquired, including documentation supporting any brand name descriptions (see 12.102). 

See Section C of RFP 

Date(s) and place(s) of delivery and acceptance and f.o.b. point (see part 47). 

A list of solicitation provisions that apply to the acquisition (see 12.205(a)). See Section L of RFP  

A list of contract clauses that apply to the acquisition (see 12.205(b)). See Section I of RFP 

The date, time, and place for receipt of offer and point of contact. 

See 1.6 Proposal Due Date of RFP 

Any other additional information required by 5.101(c). 

None

opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation and Request for Quote (RFQ) for commercial items in accordance with the procedures of Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 12 - Acquisition of Commercial Items and FAR Part 13 - Simplified Acquisition Procedures, with FAR Part 12 taking precedence per FAR 13.500(b)’s reference to FAR 12.102(c). This announcement constitutes the only solicitation with quotes requested. This solicitation is being posted SUBJECT TO THE AVAILABILITY OF FUNDS.

The Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) is seeking contractor services to: (1) develop and deliver commercially available quarterly reports for comparing loan origination and prepayment performance of mortgages guaranteed by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac on a firm-fixed-price (FFP) basis. The reports shall be based on commercially available and marketed data that is available to the Contractor. FHFA will use these reports internally and may share them with other government agencies or the public.

Please see attached documents for complete details.

rex_analysis.what_they_need: NOAA's vehicle for Science, Engineering, and Technical Support Services across its research offices — weather, oceans, fisheries, atmospheric research. Task orders span scientific program support, systems engineering, data management, IT systems, and environmental compliance across ~235 NOAA facilities.
rex_analysis.rex_take: Clarity24 JV has $7M in confirmed NOAA delivery (cloud migration, app modernization), which is real past performance. But SETSS is primarily a science and engineering vehicle — meteorology, oceanography, fisheries, facilities. BLN24's lanes (HCD, comms, data analytics) are narrow within this IDIQ. Best path is monitoring for specific task orders in digital/data/comms scope rather than pursuing the IDIQ prime. Due Jan 2027 — early awareness only.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Watch for task order RFPs under the existing SETSS vehicle that align with digital, UX, or data analytics scope. Clarity24's NOAA relationship is the asset here. Avoid chasing the IDIQ prime unless a teaming partner with deep NOAA scientific credentials is in place.

opps.description:
6/3/2026 UPDATE - SOLICITATION EXTENDED TO FRIDAY, JUNE 12, 2026, 12:00 PM TO ALLOW FOR RESPONSES TO OFFEROR QUESTIONS AND PROVIDE UPDATED INFORMATION

5/28/2026 UPDATE - SOLICITATION EXTENDED TO THURSDAY, JUNE 4, 2026. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION SHALL BE FORTHCOMING

SOLICITATION: 19INLE26R0085 - INL Guatemala Automated Biometric Identification System (ABIS)

(PCS: 7H20 NAICS: 518210)

BACKGROUND:

The Department of State (DOS) INL Guatemala is supporting the Guatemalan Migration Institute’s modernization effort through the installation and development of a biometrics-capable migration platform.  This effort includes the design and implementation of a new migration control platform with the capacity to instantly share passenger data with U.S. authorities in certain cases.  In order to fully implement this new platform, Guatemalan authorities need to build a traveler biometric database to register and store biometric-based entry records.  This procurement seeks to obtain server capacity to register and store said records.

ADDITIONAL INSTRUCTIONS TO OFFERORS:

Each offeror shall submit a proposal that clearly and concisely describes and defines the contractor’s response to the requirements contained in the solicitation and Statement of Work.  Use of general or vague statements such as “standard procedures will be used” or “good engineering practices” will not satisfy this requirement. Simply repeating or paraphrasing the RFP requirements without substantive, meaningful discussion will not be acceptable.  Unnecessary elaboration or presentation beyond that which is sufficient to present a complete and effective proposal is not desired and may be construed as an indication of the contractor’s lack of understanding of the requirements or cost consciousness.  Elaborate artwork or visual or other presentation aids is neither necessary nor desired. The desire is for clear succinct pictorials, graphs, etc.

At a MINIMUM, proposal packa

opps.title: Annual Inspection and Repair of Fire Equipment and Systems
opps.description: **The purpose of this amendment is to remove the Missouri service location and the necessity for the Missouri licenses and extend the response date to 5 June 2026.** **The purpose of this amendment is to provide a listing of the fire protection devices and their locations.** **The purpose of this amendment is to schedule a site visit for Thursday 30 April 2026 at 9:30am CST. Please see the attached amendment for details on registering for the site visit.** **The purpose of this amendment is to incorporate a bid schedule into the solicitation.** The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Memphis District has a requirement for annual and periodic inspection and servicing of fire equipment and systems. The solicitation number is W912EQ26QA026 and is being issued as a Request for Quote (RFQ). This is a small business set aside procurement. The associated NAICS code is 541990. Response to this solicitation must be received via email by date and time indicated on solicitation; regardless of method of delivery, it is the offeror?s responsibility to ensure receipt of quote by the time and date stated therein. Quotes should be marked with solicitation number W912EQ26QA026 and addressed to Tommye Sepulveda, Phone (901) 579-3647, email: Tommye.B.Sepulveda@usace.army.mil or Kirk A. Middleton, Phone (901) 544-0786, Kirk.A.Middleton@usace.army.mil. Vendors not registered in System for Award Management Registration (SAM) database prior to award will not be eligible for award. Vendors may register for SAM by registering online at www.sam.gov. opps.title: Fort Peck Test Flow Cultural Resource Monitoring Survey
opps.description:
Pre-Solicitation Notice

W9128F26QA058





Ft. Peck Test Flow Cultural Monitoring Survey



On or about 22 May 2026, this office will issue Request for Quote (RFQ) to provide cultural resource services downstream of Fort Peck Lake, Montana, between River Miles 1771 and c. 1577.

The solicitation will close on or about 11 June 2026. This solicitation is restricted to SMALL BUSINESSES ONLY.



Project Description:

The contractor will execute a pedestrian cultural resource monitoring and evaluation of sites along the Missouri River between the Fort Peck Dam (Rive Mile 1771) and River Mile 1577. A boat will be required to access some of these locations. All fieldwork must be executed under the direct supervision of a crew chief meeting the Secretary of Interior's qualifications for the appropriate resource type (archaeology and/or historic architecture). Work within the Fort Peck Reservation will require Tribal Cultural Specialists approved by the Tribal Historic Preservation Office. Fieldwork standards will meet or exceed the published requirements of the state and land managing agency where it occurs. The contractor will be required to obtain permissions from private landowners and necessary permits and field work authorizations from other agencies, including the Bureau of Land Management and the North Dakota State Historic Preservation Office. Following completion of field work, the contractor must complete a cultural resource report meeting the Montana and North Dakota State Historic Preservation offices and all evaluations must meet these guidelines, the Secretary of the Interiors Guidelines and Standards for evaluation, and National Register Bulletin 15.



Period of Performance:

The period of performance will be a 15-month (457 day) period.



The above project description is for information only. Project scope of work is subject to change.



NAICS Code:

The Primary North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code anticipated for solicitation i

The agency has not fully digitized these records, leading to challenges in organization, management, and searchability as the volume of files increases. 

The goal of this contract is to create an integrated solution that combines digitization, data ingestion, document understanding, and a centralized certification repository to enhance investigations and historical record analysis.

opps.title: Thales FY26
opps.description: USCIS has a requirement to obtain the software license renewal for Thales Device HSM (Hardware Security Module) as a Service which is a key component of the overall device certificate automation project. An HSM is a specialized physical device designed to securely store and manage digital keys, cryptographic operations, and sensitive information. They provide a high level of protection for cryptographic processes and serve as a secure foundation for various security applications. Additionally, they offer a range of features including key generation, storage and protection, cryptographic operations (such as encryption and decryption), secure key exchange, and digital signatures. By employing tamper-resistant hardware and robust security mechanisms, HSMs help safeguard sensitive data and cryptographic keys from unauthorized access and tampering attempts. They also support compliance with regulatory standards and provide a trusted platform for building secure systems.

opps.title: Spectrum Valuation
opps.description: NTIA seeks contractor support for spectrum valuation to design and deliver a defensible, repeatable, and data driven valuation of designated radio-frequency spectrum bands to inform strategic planning, budgeting, policy development, and potential reallocation/sharing decisions. The effort will produce an analytical framework, an economic model, market analysis, operational utility assessment, and valuation outputs with clear decision support for leadership. A standardized valuation methodology—grounded in economic, technical, regulatory, and operational factors—is needed to support investment, sharing, auction participation, relocation cost analysis, and long term spectrum strategy.

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi

opps.title: Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR)
opps.description:
***5/28/26 - updated set-aside, Solicitation Release Date, and Period of Performanace***

The U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services (USCIS) has a requirement for Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR), consisting of teams to provide development, security, operations (DevSecOps), data processing and analytic services to support USCIS, Information Technology (IT) development delivery services. These services primarily provide identification of Fraud, National Security and Public Safety concerns. The teams focus will be on performing development, security, and operational support activities. The team will also perform, enhancements and refinements of algorithms as well as standardizing large-scale, complex, internal and external applications. 

All applications in the portfolio employ a baseline set of DevSecOps development postures along with USCIS required development gates, safeguards and requirements. They can be categorized into several overarching categories: Case Management, Investigation, Data Storage / Operations, Screening and Vetting, Immigration Operations, Immigration Law Enforcement, and Intelligence Operations.

*Currently performing market research*
win_reasons: Partial cloud/modernization match — Clarity24 JV has NOAA cloud delivery experience
Small business set-aside
OUT OF SCOPE: ship — not in BLN24 delivery lanes
⚠️ Scope gap: DevSecOps — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes

tasks.note: BLN24 prime — Census SRQA, MAF/TIGER, EDL all heavy data engineering
Adjacent (2/4) — Census SRQA used agile + DevSecOps principles; IRS Forms Mod used CI/CD pipelines. Not standalone DevSecOps prime but confirmed delivery within broader contracts.
BLN24 gap — no confirmed prime cybersecurity delivery; would need dedicated cyber partner

capability_matrix.fit_reason: Majority of task areas require teaming — may not be best lead

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$50050.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

opps.title: AN11--Amendment for Solicitation 36C24E26Q0043 - Survey Services    for EVOLVE for Women's Healthcare
opps.description:
Amendment for Solicitation 36C24E26Q0043   Survey Services for EVOLVE

This solicitation is being amended for the following:

1. Deadline for quotes is extended to 06/05/2026, 3:30 PM Eastern Time.

2. Answers are provided to timely received questions.


Questions and Answers

Q1: Is this a brand new contract or is/was there an incumbent performing these services? 

A1: This is a brand new contract.

Q2: Does VA have an estimate or past data on the percentage of the 3,600 completed surveys by mode - web and phone?

A2: This is a new survey; therefore, past data are not available. The goal of 3,600 completed surveys allows for a response rate as low as 24% while still achieving our desired sample size. Based on prior studies we anticipate a response rate of 40%. 

Q3: Does VA have an estimate or past data on the number of women Veterans who call 800# requesting a phone survey during Task 5?

A3: This is a brand new contract so no estimate or past data is available.

Q4: Can VA provide a copy of the survey? Will the final survey instrument be available to the vendor upon contract award? If not, please indicate when the final survey instrument is expected to be provided.

A4: The survey questions will be provided by the study team upon execution of the contract and a signed and finalized DUA.

Q5: Does the survey have open-ended questions? If yes, how many? Will VA want verbatim responses to be coded by the Vendor?

A5: No open-ended questions are included in the survey, with the exception of approximately 50 fields in which participants are invited to elaborate briefly (a few words maximum) when selecting the response of  Other. 

Q6: The SOW states:  Vendor must have a minimum of 3 years of experience setting up and managing secure texting for multimodal VA surveys for Veterans. opps.title: ASCA Expansion and Enhancement of Conformity Assessment for Medical Devices
opps.agency: Department of Health and Human Services
opps.description:
Statement of Work:  ASCA (Accreditation Scheme for Conformity Assessment) Expansion and Enhancement of Conformity Assessment for Medical Devices 
Background
The Division of Standards and Conformity Assessment Program (DSCA) supports the FDA's mission by driving the development, recognition, and appropriate use of voluntary consensus standards for medical devices, radiation-emitting products and emerging technologies. DSCA encourages medical device sponsors to use FDA-recognized voluntary consensus standards in their product submissions, as conformity to relevant standards both reduces regulatory burden and fosters quality. The voluntary Accreditation Scheme for Conformity Assessment (ASCA) Program is an accreditation scheme that capitalizes upon the increasingly prominent role that standards play in regulatory science and practice. An ASCA Pilot program was launched in 2018 and was made permanent in 2023.  The ASCA Program goals are to:
-	Streamline conformity assessment in device submissions
-	Enhance the FDA's confidence in test methods and results
-	Decrease the need for additional information related to conformance with a standard
-	Promote consistency, predictability, and efficiency in medical device review
-	Serve as a least burdensome approach to conformity assessment
Objective
The primary objective of this order is to help DSCA continue its work to support the enhancement and expansion of the ASCA program and conformity assessment as required in the MDUFA V legislation, to optimize the use of standards and encourage the development of regulatory ready standards, thereby continuing to reduce the regulatory burden on industry.  The contractor will support these efforts as outlined below.
Contractor needs to have substantial experience in data collection and statistical analyses and development of voluntary consensus standards as well as experience with FDA submissions, and areas of possible ASCA expansion including mechanical testing and experimental stress an

opps.title: PR16018614 - DoS-Sources Sought
opps.description:
The Department of State’s Office to Monitor and Combat Trafficking in Persons (TIP Office) leads the United States’ global engagement in the fight against human trafficking, partnering with foreign governments and civil society to develop and implement effective anti-trafficking strategies. The TIP Office works to fulfill the mandates of combating human trafficking globally as outlined in the Trafficking Victims Protection Act of 2000, as amended (TVPA), and has responsibility for bilateral and multilateral diplomacy, targeted foreign assistance, interagency coordination, and public engagement on trafficking in persons.



One of the roles of the TIP Office is to provide support for the U.S. government’s interagency coordinating bodies, the President’s Interagency Task Force to Monitor and Combat Trafficking in Persons (PITF) and the Senior Policy Operating Group (SPOG), both established under the TVPA.  In this role, the TIP Office supports the United States Advisory Council on Human Trafficking (Advisory Council), which was established by the Survivors of Human Trafficking Empowerment Act within the Justice for Victims of Trafficking Act enacted on May 22, 2015.  The Advisory Council is an independent, nongovernmental advisory body that advises and makes recommendations on federal anti-trafficking policies to the PITF.  The Council is appointed by the President for two-year terms and may serve up to two terms.

opps.title: National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI) High Plains Regional Weather Records Center (HPRWRC)
opps.description:
1. Introduction and Background

The mission of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s (NOAA) National Environmental Satellite, Data, and Information Service (NESDIS) is to provide and ensure timely access to global environmental data from satellites and other sources to promote, protect, and enhance the Nation's economy, security, environment, and quality of life. NESDIS acquires and manages the Nation's operational environmental satellites, provides data and information services to numerous end users, and conducts related research.

Since 1990, NCEI’s RCS program has traditionally supported Regional Weather Records Centers (RWRCs) established at Public Universities within six geographic regions. For more than forty years, Regional Centers have produced and delivered environmental data, products, information, and services – hereafter collectively termed “products and services” – and captured user needs. By transforming, tailoring, and augmenting NCEI’s products and services, the RWRCs provide a bridge between NCEI and regional user communities, including the private sector. The RWRCs support NOAA’s mission to advance understanding of long-term weather variability and impacts through user engagement and the translation of data into usable products and services for decision makers.

2. Scope of Work

A. Geographic Scope

This contract is for the High Plains Regional Weather Records Center (HPRWRC), which is composed of ND, SD, NE, KS, CO, and WY.

The contractor providing the HPRWRC capability shall be physically located in the region defined above and must serve all states, territories, and U.S.-affiliated islands in the region. An RWRC with unique capabilities may work with other RWRCs to meet user needs beyond its immediate region.

B. Program Scope

The HPRWRC shall provide products and services to address regional weather records needs and issues in the context of NCEI’s broader environm

NESDIS acquires and manages the Nation's operational environmental satellites, provides data and information services to numerous end users, and conducts related research.

Since 1990, NCEI’s RCS program has traditionally supported Regional Weather Records Centers (RWRCs) established at Public Universities within six geographic regions. For more than forty years, Regional Centers have produced and delivered environmental data, products, information, and services – hereafter collectively termed “products and services” – and captured user needs. By transforming, tailoring, and augmenting NCEI’s products and services, the RWRCs provide a bridge between NCEI and regional user communities, including the private sector. The RWRCs support NOAA’s mission to advance understanding of long-term weather variability and impacts through user engagement and the translation of data into usable products and services for decision makers.

2. Scope of Work

A. Geographic Scope

This contract is for the Southeast Regional Weather Records Center (SERWRC), which is composed of: VA, NC, SC, GA, AL, FL, Puerto Rico (PR), and US Virgin Islands (USVI).

The contractor providing the SERWRC capability to a region shall be physically located within the region and must serve all states, territories, and U.S.-affiliated islands in the region. An RWRC with unique capabilities may work with other RWRCs to meet user needs beyond its immediate region.

B. Program Scope

The SERWRC shall provide products and services to address regional weather records needs and issues in

opps.title: Enterprise Cloud Customer Relationship Management Platform (ECCRMP) Services
opps.description:
The subject RFQ is issued to Small Business vendors for Enterprise Cloud Customer Relationship Management Platform (ECCRMP) Services. The period of performance (PoP) for the task order for a seven (7) month transition in/base period, seven (7) month option period 1, and four (4) twelve (12) month option periods.

CBP contemplates the issuance of a Firm Fixed Price (FFP) task order (TO) with in-scope Surge Support addressed through optional Labor-Hour (LH) CLIN resulting from this solicitation.  Please note that in addition to the terms and conditions of the GSA MAS IDIQ, the order will be subject to the terms and conditions in Attachment 5, Additional Provisions, Clauses, and Terms and Conditions. The basis for award will be to arrive at a best value decision, evaluating both the technical response and price quote.

For additional information on quote submission requirements, the quote evaluation process, including the factors and evaluation criteria, and quote documentation, please refer to the "Instructions to Quoters" document attached herein.

Questions/Clarifications: Any questions or clarification requests regarding any aspect of this RFQ shall be submitted by 1pm ET, Wednesday, May 06, 2026 via GSA MAS.

Quotes: Quote responses are to be submitted NLT 1pm ET, Tuesday, May 26, 2026 via GSA MAS.

Please note, funding is currently not available, and award is contingent upon funding availability.

opps.title: OpenEye Software Annual Licenses
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Financial Resources
opps.description:
This requirement supports ongoing translational science and drug discovery activities conducted by Early Translation Branch (ETB), Chemical Genomics Branch (CGB), Informatics Core (IFX), and collaborating NCATS researchers. The requirement is for the OpenEye Non-Profit Package, including BROOD, EON, FILTER, OEChem TK, OEDocking, OMEGA, OMEGA DB, pKa Prospector, QUACPAC, ROCS, SITEHOPPER, SPRUCE, SZMAP, SZYBKI, and VIDA.

The software supports:

• comparison of synthesized small molecules using NCATS-generated biological data

• molecular modeling and virtual screening

• structure-activity relationship analysis

• molecular docking, conformer generation, and lead optimization

• qHTS data mining and related cheminformatics workflows

• continuity of in-house tools built on the OpenEye platform

Salient characteristics:

• Must provide reinstatement of the existing OpenEye software subscription environment currently used by NCATS

• Must support molecular modeling, virtual screening, and structure-based and ligand-based computational chemistry workflows

• Must support comparison of chemical structures by shape, electrostatics, and related molecular properties

• Must support conformer generation, molecular preparation, docking, visualization, and analysis

• Must support structure-activity relationship analysis and compound prioritization for follow-on testing

• Must maintain compatibility with existing NCATS workflows and internally developed tools that rely on the OpenEye platform

• Must support continuity of scientific operations with minimal disruption following lapse of coverage

• Must include vendor licensing, updates, and support associated with the proprietary OpenEye package

opps.title: NASA's Construction Management Enterprise Tool (CoMET)
opps.description:
NASA Office of Strategic Infrastructure (OSI) seeks to acquire a commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) solution for capital program and project management; along with the system administration and technical support services to provide, configure, implement, and administer the configured COTS solution. The configured COTS solution shall be known as NASA's Construction Management Enterprise Tool (CoMET). All references in the scope of work to CoMET shall refer to the contractor-provided and contractor-configured COTS solution. The scope of work included within the Performance Work Statement (PWS) includes the configuration, integration, and administration of the new software-as-a-service (i.e., cloud-based) enterprise-wide solution that includes hosting, licensing, configuration, data architecture, integration, testing, training, maintenance/support services, and system administration.  Interested vendors shall submit a quote for the attached PWS.   

Any questions or comments regarding this RFQ shall cite the RFQ number and be submitted 5 days before quotes are due.   Quotes are due by the time as indicated in GSA eBuy.  

The attached documentation to this RFQ includes requirement specifications PWS and the additional terms and conditions that apply to this RFQ and subsequent order and the NASA ITPO Software and Hardware Rider. All other terms and conditions of your GSA Federal Supply Schedule contract shall apply to the resulting order.  

In responding to this RFQ, it is the quoter's responsibility to provide current, relevant, complete, and accurate information in their quotation.  Please submit your quote as a PDF document.

The Quoter shall also provide the following information:
-	Identify any prompt payment discounts being offered.
-	One electronic copy of current pricelists and all position descriptions being quoted.
-	The names, phone numbers, and email addresses of persons to be contacted for clarification of questions of a technical nature and business nature.

opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is tasked with reducing deaths, injuries, and economic losses from motor vehicle crashes. To achieve this, NHTSA relies on various traffic safety data systems that provide essential real-world crash data for research, problem identification, program development, and grant allocation. 

The Fatality Analysis Reporting System (FARS), established in 1975, is a key component that collects data on all fatal traffic crashes in the U.S., supporting numerous safety programs. This contract aims to enhance NHTSA's records-based data

opps.title: Newspaper Digitization
opps.description:
This is a onetime service for data collection and digitization. The vendor will scan and process roughly 9,640 surfaces from Eielson’s weekly newspapers, as provided by the History Office. Five hard drives of the collection will be provided in searchable digitized format. Services are to include scanning and image processing as well as reliable project management and reporting.


All original documents will be returned in same archival boxes they were originally shipped in within six months from the time the collection is received by the vendor.

                                                                                                                                                                                     

AMENDMENT 01 This amendment serves to correct issue 1&2 expanded on bellow...


	Issue 1. Date Offers Due imputed under Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) in error Amendment 01 corrects offer due date to Alaska Daylight Time (AKDT)

Once the Q&A period has closed a final summary document of all question and provided answers will be posted via Amendment 02. 

                                                                                                                                                                                 

This Amendment is an extension to date offers due to ensure proper response to all questions received. As well as providing a longer window for contractors to evaluate service requirements.


	The Q&A period has and will remain closed as of 5/20/26 12:00 PM
opps.summary:
Background
 The Eielson Air Force Base Historian's office maintains a collection of base newspapers from 1952-2012, which is a unique and invaluable historical resource currently available only in its original, fragile paper format. 
Due to age and frequent use, this collection is at high risk of permanent degradation and loss. 
The objective of this contract is to professionally digitize the most vulnerable part of the collection (approximately 1952-1979) to preserve it and make it accessible to all base personnel.

opps.title: 7A21--Computrition Software for the Veterans Healthcare System for  the Veteran's Healthcare System of the Ozarks with a base  year plus (4) year ordering periods. Fayetteville AR  72703
opps.agency: VISN 16: South Central VA Health Care Network
opps.description:
STATEMENT OF WORK
This solicitation uses a Brand Name or Equal Description of the product required. This permits prospective contractors to offer products other than those specifically referenced by brand name. All offers must work with existing equipment that has already been purchased and is currently in use at the station.

Scope of Work: Base year operation and 4 additional Option Years license for Computrition s Hospitality Suite Client/Server Software for the Period
of 6/8/2026   6/8/2027.
Location of Work: Nutrition & Food Service

Veteran s Healthcare System of the Ozarks requires a food service software system that will automate Food Service Operations and Nutrition Care Management. The software should be Windows based, user-friendly, featuring point and-click functionality and rapid data processing. It must be able to integrate with the VA VISTA system with a preconfigured standalone interface that is ready to be integrated into the hospital network. The system must be in compliance with all IT requirements to include Single Sing On capabilities.  

Food Service Operations Management: Manage standardized ingredients, scalable recipes, and menus; automate ordering and inventory; and streamline production. Admission, discharge, transfer, and diet order information need to be able to transfer, in real-time, from VISTA to the new system. It should have a unidirectional interface enabling secure transfer of information from VISTA to the new system without the risk of overwriting data, security breaches or data corruption in the VISTA system. It is used for Menu planning, Inventory, Production Planning, Forecasting, Nutritional Analysis, Purchasing, Automated ordering. Refine Management, Cost controls, and Automated HACCP compliance.  Support a tally sheet that can be used for production, plus patient-specific substitutions and date-specific nourishments.  Perpetual inventory capability; tracking and history reports by day, week, month, year, vendor, storage loc

philosophy to better meet DoD's requirements while generating savings for the Department of
Defense.  This will be a Firm-Fixed-Price contract with Time-and-Material CLINS for Travel.
